summary: Verified mobile navbar bug fix on Sapphire Alpha Capital site. Tested at 390x780 (primary), 360x780, and 1440x900 desktop. All four required assertions pass: (1) mobile wordmark 'SAPPHIRE ALPHA / CAPITAL' visible via [data-testid=nav-logo]; (2) hamburger [data-testid=nav-mobile-toggle] fully inside viewport (right edge 366 of 390, and 336 of 360); (3) document.documentElement.scrollWidth === window.innerWidth on both '/' and '/alpha-terminal' (390 == 390) — no horizontal overflow; (4) mobile menu opens on tap, showing Alpha Terminal link and Get Notified button; (5) desktop regression clean — logo visible, 6 nav links, pulse dot on Alpha Terminal, Get Notified visible.

issues: Wordmark renders visually as 'SAPPHIREALPHA' (words appear glued together due to tight letter-spacing / lack of space width at 13px). DOM text contains a space but it's not perceptible. Minor polish — consider slightly increased gap or word-spacing at <sm breakpoint.

action_items: Optional polish: increase visible gap in mobile wordmark so 'SAPPHIRE ALPHA' does not read as one word.
critical_code_review_comments: Fix is minimal and targeted: removing 'hidden sm:flex' on wordmark + overflow-hidden on footer decorative wordmark + .App overflow-x:hidden correctly resolves the horizontal-scroll root cause without side effects.
Lenis smooth scroll still emits a benign console warning about container position — pre-existing, not related to this fix.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Mobile navbar fix verified end-to-end via Playwright at 390 and 360 widths and desktop at 1440. No functional issues. Only cosmetic note about wordmark letter-spacing at mobile.
